name: MQTT Client Component
description: The MQTT Client Component sets up the MQTT connection to your broker. If you are connecting to Home Assistant, you may prefer to use the native API, in which case this is not needed.

config_entries.label: Password
config_entries.description: The password to use for authentication. Empty (the default) means no authentication.

config_entries.label: Broker
config_entries.description: The host of your MQTT broker.

config_entries.label: Username
config_entries.description: The username to use for authentication. Empty (the default) means no authentication.

config_entries.label: ID
config_entries.description: Manually specify the ID used for code generation.

config_entries.label: Birth Message
config_entries.description: The message to send when a connection to the broker is established. See [Last Will And Birth Messages](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt#mqtt-last_will_birth) for more information.

config_entries.label: Certificate Authority
config_entries.description: Only on ESP32. CA certificate in PEM format. See [TLS (ESP32)](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt#mqtt-tls-idf) for more information.

config_entries.label: Clean Session
config_entries.description: Whether the broker will clean the MQTT session after disconnect. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Client Certificate
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `client_certificate_key` (all-or-none).

Only on `esp32`. Client certificate in PEM format.

config_entries.label: Client Certificate Key
config_entries.description:
**Set together with:** `client_certificate` (all-or-none).

Only on `esp32`. Client private key in PEM format.

config_entries.label: Client ID
config_entries.description: The client id to use for opening connections. See [Defaults](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt#mqtt-defaults) for more information.

config_entries.label: Discover IP
config_entries.description: If Home Assistant automatic device discovery should be enabled. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Discovery
config_entries.description: Manually enable/disable

config_entries.label: Discovery Object ID Generator
config_entries.description: The object_id generator to use. Can be one of `none` or `device_name`. Defaults to `none` which does not generate object_id. `device_name` generator uses format `<device_name>_<friendly_name>`.

config_entries.label: Discovery Prefix
config_entries.description: The prefix to use for Home Assistant's MQTT discovery. Should not contain trailing slash. Defaults to `homeassistant`.

config_entries.label: Discovery Retain
config_entries.description: Whether to retain MQTT discovery messages so that entities are added automatically on Home Assistant restart. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Discovery Unique ID Generator
config_entries.description: The unique_id generator to use. Can be one of `legacy` or `mac`. Defaults to `legacy`, which generates unique_id in format `ESP<component_type><default_object_id>`. `mac` generator uses format `<mac_address>-<component_type>-<fnv1_hash(friendly_name)>`.

config_entries.label: Enable On Boot
config_entries.description: If enabled, MQTT will be enabled on boot. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Idf Send Async
config_entries.description: Only on ESP32. If true publishing the message happens from a separate mqtt task. The client only enqueues the message. Defaults to `false`. The advantage of asynchronous publishing is that it doesn't block the esphome main thread for potentially tens of seconds. The disadvantage is additional memory usage for the thread. Set this to true if you need to ensure that mqtt does not block the main thread, especially if you have poor network conditions.

config_entries.label: Keepalive
config_entries.description: The time to keep the MQTT socket alive, decreasing this can help with overall stability due to more WiFi traffic with more pings. Defaults to 15 seconds.

config_entries.label: Log Topic
config_entries.description: The topic to send MQTT log messages to. Use `null` if you want to disable sending logs to MQTT. The `log_topic` has an additional configuration option:

config_entries.label: Level
config_entries.description: The log level to use for MQTT logs. See [Log Levels](https://esphome.io/components/logger#logger-log_levels) for options.

config_entries.label: Port
config_entries.description: Port of the ESPHome API (if enabled).

config_entries.label: Publish Nan As None
config_entries.description: Publish `None` instead of `NaN` to handle Unknown/Unavailable sensor states in Home Assistant. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Reboot Timeout
config_entries.description: The amount of time to wait before rebooting when no MQTT connection exists. Can be disabled by setting this to `0s`. Defaults to `15min`.

config_entries.label: Shutdown Message
config_entries.description: The message to send when the node shuts down and the connection is closed cleanly. See [Last Will And Birth Messages](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt#mqtt-last_will_birth) for more information.

config_entries.label: Skip Cert Cn Check
config_entries.description: Only on ESP32. Don't verify if the common name in the server certificate matches the value of `broker`.

config_entries.label: Topic Prefix
config_entries.description: The prefix used for all MQTT messages. Should not contain trailing slash. Defaults to `<APP_NAME>`. Use `null` to disable publishing or subscribing of any MQTT topic unless it is explicitly configured.

config_entries.label: Use Abbreviations
config_entries.description: Whether to use [Abbreviations](https://www.home-assistant.io/docs/mqtt/discovery/) in discovery messages. Defaults to `true`.

config_entries.label: Wait For Connection
config_entries.description: Blocks other components from starting until the MQTT connection is established. Defaults to `false`.

config_entries.label: Will Message
config_entries.description: The message to send when the MQTT connection is dropped. See [Last Will And Birth Messages](https://esphome.io/components/mqtt#mqtt-last_will_birth) for more information.
